post.summary: Refresh access token using refresh token
post.description:
Refresh the access token using a valid refresh token.
Returns a new access token along with the existing refresh token.

Logout the current user. This endpoint is client-side only
as we use stateless JWT authentication.

The client should discard the tokens after calling this endpoint.

get.description:

Requires a valid access token.

post.description:
Request password reset: send OTP via email.

Rate limit: 20 requests per day per email+IP combination.
After 15 requests, account is locked for 24 hours.
OTP is valid for 5 minutes.

Verify OTP and reset password: create new random password and send via email.

OTP must be entered within 5 minutes after receiving the email.

post.summary: Upload and process file with chunked reading
post.description:
Main endpoint for uploading and processing URL monitoring files.

Features:
- Always uses chunked reading for memory optimization
- Automatic file size detection and optimization
- Resume capability for large files
- Progress tracking and error recovery

Supported formats: .csv, .xlsx, .xls

Preview file structure and data quality before processing.
Uses chunked reading for consistency.

Returns sample data and statistics without importing to database.

get.description: Get current file processing capabilities and system status.
Health check for file processing service

delete.description:
Cleanup temporary processing state files.
Useful for cleaning up after interrupted processing.

get.description:
Get paginated list of firms with advanced filtering options

### Null/Empty Filtering:
- `null`: Find records where field IS NULL
- `not_null`: Find records where field IS NOT NULL
- `empty`: Find records where field = '' (empty string)
- `not_empty`: Find records where field != '' AND field IS NOT NULL

### Examples:
- Find firms with no removal date: `?date_of_removal_filter=null`
- Find firms with removal date set: `?date_of_removal_filter=not_null`
- Find firms with empty URL: `?url_filter=empty`
- Find firms with URL set: `?url_filter=not_empty`

post.description:
Import signals from a CSV or XLSX file with comprehensive error handling.

Processes uploaded CSV or XLSX files containing signal data and performs bulk
creation of signals in the database. Provides detailed statistics about the
import operation including successes, failures, and duplicates.

Args:
    _: Authentication dependency (RequireAny permission level)
    file: UploadFile containing CSV or XLSX data with signal information
    db: AsyncSession for database operations

Returns:
    SignalImportResponse: Comprehensive import results containing:
        - success: Boolean indicating overall operation success
        - message: Human-readable summary of the import operation
        - total_processed: Total number of rows processed from file
        - total_created: Number of signals successfully created
        - total_errors: Number of rows that failed to import
        - total_skipped: Number of duplicate signals skipped
        - created_signals: List of successfully created signal objects
        - errors: Detailed error information for failed rows
        - skipped: Information about skipped duplicate signals
        - warning: Optional warning message for partial failures

Raises:
    AppException.BadRequest: When file is empty, invalid format, or all imports fail
    AppException.ServerError: When unexpected server errors occur during processing

Note:
    Expected file format requires 'signal_name' column (required) and
    'description' column (optional). The function validates data integrity
    and handles duplicate detection automatically.

Download a template file for signals data import.

Provides a properly formatted template with:
- Required headers (signal_name)
- Optional headers (description)
- Example data (if requested)

Get information about signals import template and existing signals.

Returns:
- Template field requirements
- Existing signals for reference
- Import guidelines
- Template download URLs

get.description:
Get paginated sports with optional resource inclusion

Role-based filtering:
- ADMIN/MANAGER: See ALL sports
- USER (CONTRIBUTOR): See ONLY sports assigned to them

Examples:
- GET /sports?page=1&page_size=10
  Returns: [{ id, league_id, match_name, region_id }]

- GET /sports?page=1&page_size=10&include=region,league
  Returns: [{ id, league_id, match_name, region_id, region: {...}, league: {...} }]

post.description:
Import sports from a CSV or XLSX file with comprehensive error handling.

Processes uploaded CSV or XLSX files containing sports data and performs bulk
creation of sports in the database. Provides detailed statistics about the
import operation including successes, failures, and duplicates.

Args:
    _: Authentication dependency (RequireAny permission level)
    file: UploadFile containing CSV or XLSX data with sports information
    db: AsyncSession for database operations

Returns:
    SportsImportResponse: Comprehensive import results containing:
        - success: Boolean indicating overall operation success
        - message: Human-readable summary of the import operation
        - total_processed: Total number of rows processed from file
        - total_created: Number of sports successfully created
        - total_errors: Number of rows that failed to import
        - total_skipped: Number of duplicate sports skipped
        - created_sports: List of successfully created sports objects
        - errors: Detailed error information for failed rows
        - skipped: Information about skipped duplicate sports
        - warning: Optional warning message for partial failures

Raises:
    AppException.BadRequest: When file is empty, invalid format, or all imports fail
    AppException.ServerError: When unexpected server errors occur during processing

Note:
    Expected file format columns:
    - match_name (required): Match description
    - region_name (required): Region name ΓÇö must exist in database
    - league (optional): League name ΓÇö must exist in DB when provided; omit/blank = no league
    - start_time (optional): ISO format YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM (defaults to 00:00)

- Available leagues for sports import
- Required and optional fields for each template type
- Template download URLs

post.description:
Import applications from a CSV or XLSX file with comprehensive error handling.

Processes uploaded CSV or XLSX files containing application data and performs bulk
creation of applications in the database. Provides detailed statistics about the
import operation including successes, failures, and duplicates.

**Expected file columns:**
| Column            | Required | Description                                                          |
|-------------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `app_name`        | Γ£à Yes   | Application name (must be unique per region)                          |
| `url`             | Γ¥î No    | Application URL                                                      |
| `region_name`    | Γ¥î No    | Region name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to set region_id                |
| `note`            | Γ¥î No    | Note/description                                                     |
| `states`          | Γ¥î No    | Application status (ACTIVE or INACTIVE)                               |

**Note:** The combination of `app_name` + `region_id` must be unique. If `region_name` is provided,
it will be resolved to `region_id`. If not provided, `region_id` will be NULL.

Returns a summary with counts of created, failed and skipped rows.

get.description:
Download a template file for applications import.

Returns a CSV or XLSX file with the correct column headers and optional example rows
to guide users on the expected import format.

**Columns in template (in order):**
- `app_name` (required) ΓÇö Application name (must be unique per region)
- `url` (optional) ΓÇö Application URL
- `region_name` (optional) ΓÇö Region name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to set region_id
- `note` (optional) ΓÇö Note/description
- `states` (optional) ΓÇö Application status (ACTIVE or INACTIVE)

**Note:** The combination of `app_name` + `region_id` must be unique.

Get information about applications import template and requirements.

Returns:
- Available regions (for region_name field)
- Required and optional fields
- Template download URLs
- Import guidelines

Get paginated list of app images with flexible filtering and advanced sorting

This endpoint provides comprehensive filtering, sorting, and pagination capabilities for app images.
It supports sorting by both direct fields and nested fields from related entities (applications, sports, leagues)
using efficient SQL JOIN operations at the database level.

**Key Features:**
- **Flexible Filtering**: Filter by application IDs, sport IDs, signal IDs, provider, bucket name, image status, etc.
- **Advanced Sorting**: Sort by direct fields or nested fields (e.g., `app_name`, `match_name`, `start_time`, `league_name`)
- **SQL JOIN Sort**: For nested fields, sorting is performed at the database level using SQL JOINs for optimal performance
- **Natural Alphabetical Sort**: String fields are sorted naturally (letters before numbers, case-insensitive)
- **Automatic Relationship Loading**: Required relationships are automatically included when sorting by nested fields
- **Pending Records Merge**: Real-time changes (creates, updates, deletes) from cache are merged with database results
- **Global Sorting**: Sorting is performed globally across all data, not just the current page

**Sorting Examples:**
- Sort by application name: `?order_by=app_name&order_desc=false`
- Sort by sport match name: `?order_by=match_name&order_desc=false`
- Sort by start time: `?order_by=start_time&order_desc=true`
- Sort by league name: `?order_by=league_name&order_desc=false`
- Sort by creation date: `?order_by=created_at&order_desc=true`

**Filtering Examples:**
- Filter by application: `?application_ids=uuid1&application_ids=uuid2`
- Filter by sport: `?sport_ids=uuid1`
- Filter by image status: `?image_status=uploaded`
- Filter by provider: `?provider=AZURE`

**Include Relationships Examples:**
- Include application: `?include=application`
- Include all relationships: `?include=application,sport,signal`
- Note: Relationships are auto-included when sorting by nested fields

**Pagination:**
- Use `page` and `page_size` parameters for pagination
- Maximum `page_size` is 1000 items per page
- Sorting is performed globally before pagination to ensure correct order across all pages

Examples:
- GET /app_images?page=1&page_size=10&include=application,sport
- GET /app_images?application_ids=uuid1&order_by=app_name&order_desc=false
- GET /app_images?order_by=match_name&order_desc=false&include=sport

parameters.name: has_sport

parameters.name: order_by

schema.description:
Field to sort by. Supports both direct fields and nested fields from related entities. For nested fields, the system automatically uses SQL JOIN for efficient database-level sorting. 

**Direct fields** (on app_image table):
- `id`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `image_url`, `provider`, `bucket_name`, `object_key`
- `application_id`, `sport_id`, `signal_id`

**Nested fields** (from related entities, uses SQL JOIN sort):
- `app_name` or `application_name` - Sort by application name (from applications table)
- `match_name` or `sport_name` - Sort by sport match name (from sports table)
- `start_time` - Sort by sport start time (from sports table)
- `league_name` - Sort by league name (from leagues table via sports)
- `league_id` - Sort by league ID (from leagues table via sports)

**Note:** When sorting by nested fields, the required relationships are automatically included. For example, sorting by `app_name` will automatically include the `application` relationship. Natural alphabetical sorting is applied for string fields (letters before numbers, case-insensitive).
schema.title: Order By

parameters.description:
Field to sort by. Supports both direct fields and nested fields from related entities. For nested fields, the system automatically uses SQL JOIN for efficient database-level sorting.

- `start_time` - Sort by sport start time (from sports table)

- `league_id` - Sort by league ID (from leagues table via sports)

parameters.name: order_desc

schema.description: Sort order direction. `true` = descending (ZΓåÆA, newest first for dates), `false` = ascending (AΓåÆZ, oldest first for dates).
schema.title: Order Desc

parameters.description: Sort order direction. `true` = descending (ZΓåÆA, newest first for dates), `false` = ascending (AΓåÆZ, oldest first for dates).

parameters.name: include

schema.description:
Comma-separated list of related resources to include in the response. Valid values: `application`, `sport`, `signal`, or any combination like `application,sport,signal`. 

**Note:** When using `order_by` with nested fields (e.g., `app_name`, `match_name`), the required relationships are automatically included even if not specified in this parameter. For example, sorting by `app_name` will automatically include `application` relationship.

get.description:
Download a template file for app images import.

Returns a CSV or XLSX file with the correct column headers and optional example rows
to guide users on the expected import format.

**Important:** When importing, use query parameter `region_id` if you want to link app images to sports:
- `POST /api/v1/app_images/import?region_id=<UUID>`

**Columns in template (in order):**
- `application_name` (required) ΓÇö Application name ΓÇö must exist in DB
- `signal_name` (optional) ΓÇö Signal name ΓÇö auto-created if not exists; leave blank for no signal
- `match_name` (optional*) ΓÇö Sport match name ΓÇö requires `start_time` (file) + `region_id` (query param) if provided
- `start_time` (optional*) ΓÇö Match start time ΓÇö required if `match_name` provided. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM, dd-mm-yyyy, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-mm-yyyy HH:MM, dd/mm/yyyy HH:MM
- `league` (optional) ΓÇö League name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to filter sport lookup (more precise matching)
- `image_url` (optional) ΓÇö Image URL ΓÇö leave empty if no file uploaded yet

**Linking to Sport:**
To link an app image to a sport, you must provide:
1. `region_id` as query parameter (e.g. `?region_id=123e4567-...`)
2. `match_name` in file column
3. `start_time` in file column
4. `league` in file column (optional, but recommended for precise matching)

System will lookup sport by exact match of: region_id + match_name + start_time + league_id (if league provided).

put.summary: Update app image by uploading a new file
put.description:
Update app image by uploading a new file.

System automatically manages:
- Uses existing bucket_name and object_key structure (keeps same location)
- Auto-generates new path if needed
- Deletes old file from storage
- Updates all metadata automatically (NO DELETE + CREATE, just UPDATE)

User only needs to upload the new file - system handles everything else.

Args:
    image_id: ID of the app image to update
    file: New file to upload
    provider: Optional new storage provider (only if changing provider)
    bulk: If True, uses bulk upload queue for better performance

post.summary: Import app images from CSV or XLSX file
post.description:
Import app images from a CSV or XLSX file.

Processes uploaded file and bulk-creates app image records.
For each row, optional FK fields are resolved by name via get_smart (cached):
- `application_name` + `application_url` (optional) + `region_id` (optional) ΓåÆ looks up application ΓåÆ sets `application_id`
- `signal_name` ΓåÆ looks up signal ΓåÆ sets `signal_id` (auto-creates if not exists)
- `match_name` + `start_time` + `region_id` ΓåÆ looks up sport ΓåÆ sets `sport_id`

**Query Parameters:**
- `region_id`: UUID of region (optional, can be provided in file via `region_id` column; required if `match_name` is provided in file)

**Expected file columns:**
| Column            | Required | Description                                                          |
|-------------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `application_name`| Γ£à Yes   | Application name ΓÇö must exist in DB                                   |
| `application_url`| Γ¥î No    | Application URL ΓÇö used to distinguish applications with same name. If provided, lookup uses (app_name, region_id, url). If not provided or empty, lookup uses (app_name, region_id, url=None) |
| `region_id`      | Γ¥î No    | Region ID (UUID) ΓÇö used to distinguish applications with same name. If provided in file, overrides query parameter. If not provided, uses query parameter `region_id` or None |
| `signal_name`     | Γ¥î No    | Signal name ΓÇö auto-created if not exists; omit to leave signal empty |
| `match_name`      | Γ¥î No    | Sport match name ΓÇö requires `start_time` and `region_id` if provided |
| `start_time`      | Γ¥î No    | Match start time ΓÇö required if `match_name` provided. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM, dd-mm-yyyy, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-mm-yyyy HH:MM, dd/mm/yyyy HH:MM |
| `league`          | Γ¥î No    | League name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to filter sport lookup (more precise) |
| `image_url`       | Γ¥î No    | Image URL ΓÇö leave empty if no file uploaded yet                       |

**Application Lookup Logic:**
- If `application_url` is provided: lookup by (app_name, region_id, url)
- If `application_url` is empty/None: lookup by (app_name, region_id, url=None)
- `region_id` can be provided in file column or query parameter (file column takes precedence)
- Application must exist in DB before import (no auto-creation)

**Note:** To link an app image to a sport, you must provide:
- `region_id` (query parameter or file column)
- `match_name` (file column)
- `start_time` (file column)
- `league` (file column, optional but recommended for precise matching)

System will lookup sport by: region_id + match_name + start_time + league_id (if league provided).

Returns a summary with counts of created, failed and skipped rows.

parameters.name: region_id

schema.description: Region ID (optional, can be provided in file via 'region_id' column; required if match_name is provided in file)
schema.title: Region Id

parameters.description: Region ID (optional, can be provided in file via 'region_id' column; required if match_name is provided in file)

get.description:
Get paginated list of detected links with filtering options

- Admin (admin:all) and Managers (group:manage): See all detected links
- Regular users: See only their own detected links (assigned_user_id = current_user.id)

**Filtering by image:**
- `has_image=true` - Only detected links with images
- `has_image=false` - Only detected links without images
- `image_status=uploading` - Only detected links with images being uploaded (image_url starts with 'pending_')
- `image_status=uploaded` - Only detected links with uploaded images (image_url not null and not 'pending_')

Examples:
- GET /detected_links?page=1&page_size=10
  Returns: [{ id, url, signal_id, sport_id, assigned_user_id }]

- GET /detected_links?page=1&page_size=10&include=signal,sport,detected_link_image
  Returns: [{ id, url, signal_id, sport_id, assigned_user_id,
             signal: {...}, sport: {...}, detected_link_image: {...} }]

- GET /detected_links?has_image=true&image_status=uploaded
  Returns: Only detected links with uploaded images

- GET /detected_links?order_by=image_url&order_desc=false
  Returns: Detected links sorted by image URL (nulls last)

post.summary: Import detected links from CSV or XLSX file
post.description:
Import detected links from a CSV or XLSX file.

Processes uploaded file and bulk-creates detected link records.
For each row, optional FK fields are resolved by name via get_smart (cached):
- `signal_name` ΓåÆ looks up signal ΓåÆ sets `signal_id`
- `match_name` + `start_time` + `region_id` ΓåÆ looks up sport ΓåÆ sets `sport_id`
- `domain_id` is auto-derived from the URL (same as single-create flow).

**Query Parameters:**
- `region_id`: UUID of region (required if `match_name` is provided in file)

**Expected file columns:**
| Column            | Required | Description                                                          |
|-------------------|----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `url`             | Γ£à Yes   | The URL to import                                                    |
| `match_name`      | Γ¥î No    | Sport match name ΓÇö requires `start_time` and `region_id` if provided |
| `start_time`      | Γ¥î No    | Match start time ΓÇö required if `match_name` provided. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM, dd-mm-yyyy, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-mm-yyyy HH:MM, dd/mm/yyyy HH:MM |
| `league`          | Γ¥î No    | League name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to filter sport lookup (more precise) |
| `signal_name`     | Γ¥î No    | Signal name ΓÇö must exist in DB; omit to leave signal empty           |
| `social_media_type`| Γ¥î No  | Social media type (e.g. "web", "facebook") ΓÇö must exist in DB; omit to leave empty |
| `view`            | Γ¥î No    | Integer view count; leave empty for NULL                              |

**Note:** To link a detected link to a sport, you must provide:
- `region_id` (query parameter)
- `match_name` (file column)
- `start_time` (file column)
- `league` (file column, optional but recommended for precise matching)

System will lookup sport by: region_id + match_name + start_time + league_id (if league provided).

Returns a summary with counts of created, failed and skipped rows.

schema.description: Region ID (required if match_name is provided in file)
schema.title: Region Id

parameters.description: Region ID (required if match_name is provided in file)

get.description:
Download a template file for detected links import.

Returns a CSV or XLSX file with the correct column headers and optional example rows
to guide users on the expected import format.

**Important:** When importing, use query parameter `region_id` if you want to link detected links to sports:
- `POST /api/v1/detected_links/import?region_id=<UUID>`

**Columns in template (in order):**
- `url` (required) ΓÇö The URL to detect/import
- `match_name` (optional*) ΓÇö Sport match name ΓÇö requires `start_time` (file) + `region_id` (query param) if provided
- `start_time` (optional*) ΓÇö Match start time ΓÇö required if `match_name` provided. Supported formats: YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM, dd-mm-yyyy, dd/mm/yyyy, dd-mm-yyyy HH:MM, dd/mm/yyyy HH:MM
- `league` (optional) ΓÇö League name ΓÇö must exist in DB; used to filter sport lookup (more precise matching)
- `signal_name` (optional) ΓÇö Signal name ΓÇö must exist in DB; leave blank for no signal
- `social_media_type` (optional) ΓÇö Social media type (e.g. "web", "facebook") ΓÇö **Auto-detected from domain** if not provided: "facebook" if domain contains "facebook", else "web". You can override by providing value in file.
- `view` (optional) ΓÇö Integer view count; leave empty for NULL

**Linking to Sport:**
To link a detected link to a sport, you must provide:
1. `region_id` as query parameter (e.g. `?region_id=123e4567-...`)
2. `match_name` in file column
3. `start_time` in file column
4. `league` in file column (optional, but recommended for precise matching)

System will lookup sport by exact match of: region_id + match_name + start_time + league_id (if league provided).

delete.description:
Delete a detected Links

- Admin (admin:all) and Managers (group:manage): Can delete any detected link
- Regular users: Can only delete their own detected links (assigned_user_id = current_user.id)

Args:
    DetectedLinks_id: ID of the detected link to delete
    bulk: If True, uses write-behind pattern (adds to pending operations for batch processing).
           If False, deletes directly from database (write-through).

post.summary: Upload file (multipart) and create detected link image record
post.description:
Endpoint for tools / third-party to upload file and create record.
Supports AZURE and GOOGLE_CLOUD providers.
Accepts any file type: images, excel, csv, pdf, etc.

put.summary: Update detected link image by uploading a new file
put.description:
Update detected link image by uploading a new file.

System automatically manages:
- Uses existing bucket_name and object_key structure (keeps same location)
- Auto-generates new path if needed (date/sportname/filename)
- Deletes old file from storage
- Updates all metadata automatically

User only needs to upload the new file - system handles everything else.

- Admin (admin:all) and Managers: Can update any image
- Regular users: Can only update images from detected_links assigned to them

Args:
    image_id: ID of the detected link image to update
    file: New file to upload
    provider: Optional new storage provider (only if changing provider)
    bulk: If True, uses bulk upload queue for better performance

post.description: Start auto-push background service for all cache managers
≡ƒ¢æ Stop auto-push background service for all cache managers
ΓÜí Manually force push all pending operations to database
ΓÜí Force push pending operations for specific manager

≡ƒöº Reset items stuck in 'processing' status or blocked by retry_after back to 'pending' so they can be retried.

Use when workers crash and items get stuck in 'processing' state or blocked by retry_after.

Options:
- Default: Reset items stuck longer than max_processing_seconds (60s default)
- reset_all_non_done=True: Reset ALL items except 'done' (useful for force push)

post.description:
Send sport report email with data from database

This endpoint:
- Extracts domain from detected_links
- Gets all URLs for the domain
- Gets sport information (match, time, date)
- Renders report_sport template with data
- Sends email with header and footer images

Features:
- Auto-forward to nguyen.kiet@phan.vn (via BCC)
- Auto-backup to Google Drive
- Uses Letter head-Web-01.png and Letter footer-Web-01.png

get.description:
Preview sport report email template with data from database

This endpoint:
- Gets data from detected_links and sports tables
- Renders template with actual data
- Returns HTML preview (does not send email)

Use this to test and preview the email before sending.

post.summary: Send test email with file attachments
post.description:
Send a test email with file attachments

This endpoint allows you to:
- Upload multiple files (Excel, PDF, images, etc.)
- Send email with attachments
- Test both Gmail API and SMTP methods
- Automatic file size validation (Gmail limit: 25MB)

Supported file types:
- Excel files (.xlsx, .xls)
- PDF files (.pdf)
- Images (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif)
- Text files (.txt, .csv)
- Word documents (.doc, .docx)
- Any other file type

The system will automatically detect MIME type based on file extension.

ImportErrorDetail.title: ImportErrorDetail
ImportErrorDetail.description:
Detailed information about import operation failures.

Provides comprehensive error context for debugging failed row imports,
including the exact data that caused the failure and the specific error message.

Attributes:
    row: Zero-based row number in the imported file where the error occurred
    data: Dictionary containing the raw data from the failed row
    error: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong

Example:
    >>> error_detail = ImportErrorDetail(
    ...     row=5,
    ...     data={"signal_name": "", "description": "Invalid signal"},
    ...     error="Signal name cannot be empty"
    ... )

example.message: Processed 10 rows, created 8 items, skipped 2 duplicates
Processed 10 rows, created 8 items, skipped 2 duplicates
Processed 10 rows, created 8 items, skipped 2 duplicates

ImportSkippedDetail.title: ImportSkippedDetail
ImportSkippedDetail.description:
Information about rows skipped during import operations.

Tracks items that were not imported due to business logic constraints
such as duplicate detection or validation rules, providing transparency
about what was excluded and why.

Attributes:
    item_name: The name/identifier of the item that was skipped
    reason: Explanation of why the item was not imported

Example:
    >>> skipped_detail = ImportSkippedDetail(
    ...     item_name="Trading Alert",
    ...     reason="Item already exists in database"
    ... )
